footnotes: 3. Participants will be admitted to the CRU for Treatment Period 1 and Treatment Period 2 when in the CRU, through Day 39.
4. The CRU’s discretion, participants may remain in the CRU or be readmitted on Day 22 with all procedures starting on Day 23.
5. Unscheduled study visits may occur at any time during the study and may include any study procedure as deemed necessary by the Investigator.
6. Participants are required to return to the CRU Day 54+/_2; participants will be discontinued from ALXN1840 medication by their physician at the latest by the Day 54 follow_up.

7. Discharge from the unit may occur after completion of all procedures on Day 9 and on Day 40 and after the Investigator has reviewed all safety assessment (including safety laboratory tests) and confirmed that the participant is appropriate for discharge.

8. A single outpatient visit or phone call and safety laboratory assessment should occur between Day 14 and Day 18. A phone call may take place on a different day than the blood draw within the Day 14 through Day 18 period.

data.name: Primary Objective: Net Copper Balance
data.text: Assess net copper balance with daily repeat_dose ALXN1840 treatment (15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with Wilson disease (WD)

data.name: Secondary Objective: Change in Copper Balance
data.text: Assess change in copper balance in response to ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day during ALXN1840 accumulation and at steady state periods versus the pretreatment baseline in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Copper Disposition
data.text: Investigate the effect of ALXN1840 (15 mg/day and 30 mg/day) on the disposition of copper in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Molybdenum Disposition
data.text: Investigate the effect of ALXN1840 on the disposition of molybdenum at steady state at 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Steady_State Molybdenum Balance
data.text: Assess steady_state total molybdenum balance as a measure of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Molybdenum Accumulation
data.text: Assess accumulation of molybdenum with ALXN1840 treatment at 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Molybdenum PK
data.text: Determine the steady_state plasma pharmacokinetic (PK) of total molybdenum and plasma ultrafiltrate (PUF) molybdenum (as surrogate measures of ALXN1840, 15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with WD

data.name: Secondary Objective: Safety and Tolerability
data.text: Evaluate the safety and tolerability of repeated_dose administration of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Dose Response for Copper Balance
data.text: Determine dose response of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day for copper balance in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Effect of Treatment Duration on Copper Balance
data.text: Determine the effect of treatment duration on copper balance in participants with WD

data.name: Exploratory Objective: Ceruloplasmin and LBC Profiles
data.text: Assess the effects of ALXN1840 on ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin_bound copper LBC profiles in plasma in participants with WD

data.name: Age requirement
data.text: Participants aged ≥18 at the time of signing the ICF.

data.name: Diagnosis of WD
data.text: Diagnosis of WD by Leipzig Criteria ≥ 4 documented by testing as outlined in the 2012 European Association for the Study of Liver WD Clinical Practice Guidelines (Ferenci, 2003; EASL, 2012) or by historical test results for WD including some or all of the following: • Presence of Kayser Fleischer rings, • Neurological symptoms, • Serum ceruloplasmin below reference range, • Coombs_negative hemolytic anemia, • Elevated liver or urinary copper, • Presence of mutations in the ATP7B gene, or • Other, as considered appropriate, may be used instead to confirm the diagnosis of WD.

data.name: Benefit from decoppering therapy
data.text: Participants who in the opinion of the referring Investigator may benefit from decoppering therapy.

data.name: Comply with procedures
data.text: Participants must be able to comply with all study_related procedures.

data.name: Reside in CRU
data.text: Participants must be able to reside in the CRU for intensive metabolic monitoring of copper and molybdenum.

data.name: Discontinue chelator therapy
data.text: Participants willing to discontinue chelator therapy for approximately 4 days prior to initiation of ALXN1840 to allow a baseline assessment of copper balance.

data.name: Adequate venous access
data.text: Participants with adequate venous access to allow collection of required blood samples.

data.name: Swallow intact tablets
data.text: Participants must be able to swallow intact ALXN1840 tablets.

data.name: Avoid certain minerals
data.text: Participants willing to avoid use of minerals containing copper, zinc, or molybdenum throughout the study duration.

data.name: Adhere to controlled diet
data.text: Participants willing to adhere to copper/molybdenum_controlled diet during inpatient periods and willing to comply with a low copper dietary requirement during the Outpatient Period.

data.name: Contraceptive use
data.text: Contraceptive use by men or women should be consistent with local regulations regarding the methods of contraception for those participating in clinical studies. a. Male participants: • Male participants, if heterosexually active and with a female spouse or partner of childbearing potential or a pregnant or breastfeeding spouse or partner, must agree to use barrier contraception (male condom) for the duration of the study and for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Male participants must not donate sperm for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female spouses or partners of male participants who are of childbearing potential must use highly effective contraception as defined below and in Section 10.4, starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before (the male participant’s) first study intervention administration and continuing until at least 3 months after the end of their male partner’s systemic exposure to the study intervention. • Barrier contraception (male condom) is required even with documented medical assessment of surgical success of a vasectomy. For male participants who have had a vasectomy (with documented evidence of azoospermia if possible) and agree to use a male condom for the stated time period, no additional contraceptive method is required by their female partner. b. Female participants: • Female participants or female partners of male participants of childbearing potential (including breastfeeding females), if heterosexually active, must be willing to follow protocol_specified contraception guidance starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before first study intervention administration and continuing for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Female participants must not donate ova for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female participants who are documented as being of non_childbearing potential as defined in Section 10.4 are exempt from contraception requirements. • Highly effective contraceptive methods for female participants and female partners of male participants are described in Section 10.4.

data.name: Signed informed consent
data.text: Participants capable of giving signed informed consent, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICF and in this protocol.

data.name: Decompensated cirrhosis or MELD score
data.text: Decompensated cirrhosis or model for end_stage liver disease (MELD) score > 13.

data.name: Modified Nazer score
data.text: Modified Nazer score > 7 (Dhawan, 2005).

data.name: Gastrointestinal bleed
data.text: Clinically significant gastrointestinal bleed within past 3 months.

data.name: Elevated ALT
data.text: Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) > 2 × upper limit of normal (ULN).

data.name: Marked neurological disease
data.text: Marked neurological disease requiring assistance with self_care or activities of daily life.

data.name: Low hemoglobin
data.text: Hemoglobin less than lower limit of the reference range for age and sex.

data.name: Active infections (HBV, HCV, HIV)
data.text: Active infection with hepatitis B virus (positive hepatitis B surface antigen) or C virus (participants with positive hepatitis C antibody result would require confirmation of active disease with a positive hepatitis C polymerase chain reaction test), or seropositivity for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).

data.name: Significant medical history
data.text: History or presence of/significant history of or current cardiovascular, respiratory, hepatic, renal, gastrointestinal, endocrinological, hematological, or neurological disorders, or psychiatric disorder that in the opinion of the Investigator may constitute a risk when taking the study intervention; or may interfere with the interpretation of data.

data.name: Previous ALXN1840 or tetrathiomolybdate
data.text: Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or other form of tetrathiomolybdate within 1 year prior to dosing.

data.name: Previous zinc treatment
data.text: Previous treatment with zinc within 21 days prior to Day 1.

data.name: Experimental or unapproved therapy
data.text: The use of an experimental or unapproved/unlicensed therapy at the same time or within 90 days or 5 half_lives, whichever is longer, prior to the Screening Visit.

data.name: Renal failure
data.text: Participants in renal failure, defined as in end_stage renal disease on dialysis (chronic kidney disease [CKD] stage 5) or creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min (Levey, 2006).

data.name: Pregnant or breastfeeding
data.text: Pregnant (or females who are planning to become pregnant) or breastfeeding females (women of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test result at screening).

data.name: Known sensitivity to ALXN1840
data.text: Known sensitivity to ALXN1840, ALXN1840 excipients (anhydrous dicalcium phosphate, anhydrous sodium carbonate), or any of the ingredients contained in ALXN1840 or related compounds.

data.name: Non_compliant or uncooperative
data.text: In the opinion of the Investigator, the participant and/or their legal guardian is likely to be non_compliant or uncooperative during the study.

data.name: History of substance abuse
data.text: History of illicit drug abuse, history of significant alcohol abuse within 1 year prior to the Screening Visit, or clinical evidence of substance and/or alcohol abuse within the 2 years before screening. Alcohol abuse is defined as regular weekly intake of more than 14 units (for both males and females), using the following National Health Service (NHS) alcohol tracker http://www.nhs.uk/Tools/Pages/drinks_tracker.aspx.

data.name: Positive urine drug screen
data.text: Positive urine drug toxicology screen at Screening or on Day _8 (cannabinoids will not be tested).

data.name: Recent alcohol consumption
data.text: Alcohol consumption within 48 hours prior to study intervention administration or positive alcohol breath test at screening or on Day _8.

data.name: Unwilling to complete meals
data.text: Participants unwilling to consistently complete every meal and tolerate a controlled, limited menu for the duration of the study.

data.name: Screening to Enrollment/Run_in Transition
data.text: Participants eligible for the study will be enrolled on Day _7 after meeting all inclusion/exclusion criteria and signing ICF. Participants are registered and assigned a participant number.

data.name: Run_in to Treatment Period 1 Transition
data.text: Following the Run_in Period (Day _7 through Day _1), participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day starting Day 1. Chelator therapy must be discontinued approximately 4 days prior and zinc therapy at least 21 days prior to Day 1.

data.name: Inpatient Period 1 to Outpatient Period Transition
data.text: Participants may be discharged from the CRU on Day 9 to return on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose). At the CRU's discretion, participants may remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period to Inpatient Period 2 Transition
data.text: Participants return to CRU on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose); all procedures will start on Day 23. Outpatient Period may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval; participant will continue Inpatient Period 2 at Day 23.

data.name: Treatment Period 1 to Treatment Period 2 Dose Titration
data.text: ALXN1840 dose increased from 15 mg/day to 30 mg/day on Day 29. Before titration, the SRC will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant. If safety concerns exist, participants may remain on 15 mg/day for the duration of the study.

data.name: Treatment Period 2 to Follow_up/EOS Transition
data.text: Following completion of both inpatient periods (Day 40), participants transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or consider continuation of ALXN1840. All participants should return for EOS Visit on Day 54 +/_ 2 days.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN requires dose reduction to previous dose level if up_titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality. Weekly repeat testing required.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for ALT > 5x ULN
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when ALT is below 3 × ULN.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hemoglobin Decrease > 20%
data.text: Hemoglobin > 20% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Platelet Count Decrease > 25%
data.text: Platelet count > 25% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Neutrophil Count Decrease > 30%
data.text: Neutrophil count > 30% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN indicative of liver injury requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when bilirubin is below ULN, requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Neurological Worsening Dose Evaluation
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or neurologic physical exam assessment: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale must be documented and re_evaluated at next study visit.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening Dose Evaluation
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale must be documented and re_evaluated at next study visit.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Use of Disallowed Medication
data.text: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Abnormal Liver Function
data.text: Discontinuation of study intervention for abnormal liver function should be considered by the Investigator when a participant meets one of the conditions outlined in the Dose Modification criteria (Section 6.6) or if the Investigator believes it is in the best interest of the participant.

data.name: Discontinuation by Investigator or Alexion Determination
data.text: Alexion or the Investigator deems it is necessary for the participant. Investigators must notify Alexion immediately of study intervention discontinuation. The decision to discontinue should not be delayed for causality assessment.

data.name: Participant Voluntary Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request. The participant will be permanently discontinued both from the study intervention and from the study at that time.

data.name: Investigator_Initiated Withdrawal
data.description: Participant may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioral, compliance, or administrative reasons.

data.name: Study Intervention Discontinuation with Safety Follow_up
data.description: If study intervention is definitively discontinued, the participant should remain in the study to be evaluated for safety follow_up. An early discontinuation visit should be conducted as shown in the SoA.

data.name: Lost to Follow_up
data.description: A participant will be considered lost to follow_up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site. At least 3 telephone calls and a certified letter must be attempted before deeming lost to follow_up.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: A participant is considered to have completed the study if he/she has completed all phases of the study including the last scheduled procedure shown in the SoA. End of Study Visit on Day 54 +/_ 2 days.

data.name: Study Termination by Alexion
data.description: Alexion reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of Alexion. Reasons may include failure to comply with protocol, inadequate recruitment, or discontinuation of further study intervention development.

data.name: SRC Safety Review for Dose Titration Decision
data.description: Before titration to 30 mg/day on Day 29, the Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant. If safety concerns exist, participants may remain on 15 mg/day for the duration of the study.

data.name: ALT_Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose reduction (ALT > 3x ULN) or temporary interruption (ALT > 5x ULN) based on liver function test results. Weekly repeat testing required.

data.name: Liver Injury Assessment Decision (Bilirubin + ALT)
data.description: Decision point for temporary interruption when bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN indicative of liver injury. Rechallenge requires Alexion Medical Monitor approval.

data.name: Neurological Worsening Assessment Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose modification evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data when neurologic worsening is observed.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening Assessment Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose modification evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data when clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening is observed.

data.name: Rechallenge Limit Decision
data.description: Decision point to determine if rechallenge is permissible. A maximum of 3 rechallenges are allowed. Participants on 15 mg QOD should be rechallenged at 15 mg QOD dose.

data.name: CRU Discharge vs Continued Inpatient Decision
data.description: At the CRU's discretion, participants may be discharged on Day 9 or remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period Extension Decision
data.description: The Outpatient Period during Treatment Period 1 may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval. Additional investigational product will be provided for daily dosing.

data.name: Post_Study Therapy Decision
data.description: Following completion of both inpatient periods (Day 40), participants will either transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or at the discretion of the treating physician consider continuation of ALXN1840 under preapproval access.

data.name: QTcF Clinical Significance Decision
data.description: If a clinically significant finding in QTcF is identified after enrollment, the Investigator determines if the participant can continue in the study and if any change in participant management is needed.
